findings.description: Per file_schemas Coverage-Required Fields table, every sections[].charter and sections[].acceptance must have at least one assertion whose `covers` field points back to it. The MCA.ui-shell.* assertions (1-6) all `covers` ledger first_contact_requirements (FC.1/FC.2/FC.3/FC.10) rather than the ui-shell section itself. The ui-shell charter is functionally fully exercised — input/PAT/button/link/console/repeat-lookup — but the `covers` graph does not contain a direct edge to `#sections[id=ui-shell]`. Substantive coverage is in place via FC.* edges; this is a graph-walk hygiene gap, not a missing-test gap.
findings.recommended_action: Optionally add `#sections[id=ui-shell]` as an additional `covers` target on one MCA.ui-shell.* entry, or accept that FC.*-coverage transitively addresses the charter. Note: same pattern applies less strictly to api-client (MCA.api-client.1 covers it directly), card-renderer (MCA.card-renderer.7 covers it), data-derivers (MCA.data-derivers.1/.4 cover it), and edge-case-testing (MCA.edge-case-testing.1/.2 cover it). ui-shell is the only section whose own charter id is not a `covers` target.

findings.description: Per file_schemas, each sections[].out_of_scope[] item is coverage-required. The per-section OOS items (api-client: 4 items; data-derivers: 3; ui-shell: 4; card-renderer: 3; edge-case-testing: 3) are not individually addressed by `covers` pointers. Ledger-level OOS items ARE all covered (DCA.OOS.* × 12). The section-level OOS items are tighter-scoped scope-fences (e.g., 'no caching' for api-client) and would only be violated if the implementation drifted; static-checks already structurally rule out the named violations (no caching APIs used, no DOM access in api-client/data-derivers, no auto-headless-browser code). Effective coverage is in place; explicit `covers` edges are not.
findings.recommended_action: Optionally extend MCA per-section assertions with additional `covers` entries pointing at the section-level OOS items, or accept the static-checks results as functional coverage. Low-severity hygiene.

findings.description: Per file_schemas Coverage-Required Fields, contracts/original/*.json `interface` (each method/field) and `notes` are coverage-required. No `covers` field in the assertion graph points at any contracts/original/*.json#interface or #notes path. The MCA.api-client.* assertions verify the implementation (signature, error names, GraphQL body shape, Authorization header form) which functionally validates the contract's exports[] and invariants[], but the `covers` edges target sections[] and ledger fields rather than the contract files themselves. This is the most consequential coverage-map gap in the build — under a strict graph walk it would read as 'contracts uncovered', though every contract claim is exercised by an assertion in practice.
findings.recommended_action: For the v1.10 candidate amendment loop: either (a) extend the `covers` field semantics to permit contract-path targets and have TD emit one MCA per contract export pointing at it, OR (b) document in file_schemas that contract interfaces are coverage-discharged by the section MCA chain. For this build: no rectification needed — every contract export has a corresponding implementation-side MCA that passes. Flagging for architectural awareness, not for build re-work.

caveat_rationale: All caveats trace to the user-supplied PAT, which is intrinsic to the artifact's evidence-backed IP1 default branch (PAT required for GraphQL pinnedItems + contributionsCollection). Modeled per Principle H using canonical-evidence fixtures grounded in research/probes/probe-quick-github-api/findings.json.
